notes: Prework is a setup artefact, not a numbered module — Module File Shape's Big Idea / Key Concepts / What You'll Learn sections do not apply (§§5/6/7 N/A). Strategy doc carries no dedicated 'prework Big Idea' line; the implicit Big Idea ('Land at M1 with Claude Code started in the repo you'll grow, curated skills installed, one trivial bug picked') is restated nearly verbatim in the file's opening orientation and matches strategy-doc lines 270 + 665 + 332. Mood is setup-exempt per §1's diagnostic: every step here is plumbing (open the tool, pick the repo, start a session, download, hand off). Strategy-fidelity §4 is the load-bearing gate: file installs the two curated skills (access-control-analysis + stride) WITHOUT invoking them or lecturing security — preserves M3's earn-the-trust teaching moment. Theme presence: 'compounding' surfaces once as engineering verb ('which repo are you going to grow rules and memory on across the training'); themes are seeded experientially per Recurring themes § line 106 ('compounded module by module'). Engineer-register peer voice ('Your call.', 'A picked bug from Step 4. Write it down.') matches AE101 voice quintet. Maintainer block discloses CONTENT_URL placeholder as deliberate carve-out to check_prompts.md §1 (build-time substitution) — not a strategy concern. Closing Connections question ('what's one trick you figured out with Claude Code that nobody taught you?') primes M1's joyful-creation mood without resolving it.
